name: Fast Credit Financial
description_short: Fast Business Financial provides small business loans and funding solutions with instant application processing and rapid approval decisions for businesses of varying sizes.
description_long:
Fast Business Financial is a business financing company headquartered in Los Angeles, California that specializes in small business lending. The company operates through a streamlined digital platform accessible via their website, allowing business owners to apply for funding online or by phone. They position themselves as a rapid-approval lender with a focus on same-day or next-day funding decisions. The company employs loan specialists who guide applicants through a multi-step qualification process based on business metrics like years in operation, monthly revenue, and desired funding amount.

Fast Business Financial offers small business loans across a wide range of funding tiers, from $1,000 up to over $500,000. Their application process is segmented into a 3-step form that collects basic business and personal information, company details, funding needs, years in business, and monthly revenue figures. They appear to market primarily through digital channels and provide phone and text-based customer support. The company accepts applications from businesses with less than one year of operating history through those with 7+ years of experience.

The company distinguishes itself through aggressive speed-to-funding messaging ("GET FUNDED TODAY") and a personalized loan specialist assignment model. They offer convenient multi-channel application (online form, phone consultation, text opt-in messaging). Their qualification criteria explicitly acknowledge businesses that don't yet generate revenue or have been operating less than one year, suggesting they may serve higher-risk borrowers than traditional lenders. The application explicitly discloses automatic qualification denials for certain applicant profiles based on funding amount and revenue thresholds.

Based on available information, Fast Business Financial appears to operate as a marketplace lender or alternative business financing provider. However, the website provides limited transparency regarding actual loan terms, interest rates, fees, repayment periods, or specific loan product types (SBA loans, merchant cash advances, equipment financing, lines of credit, etc.). No third-party reviews, BBB ratings, or regulatory compliance information is visible on the contact page. Borrowers should research this company's licensing, consumer complaint history, and actual loan terms before applying.
address: 1730 W Olympic Blvd #102 D, Los Angeles, CA 90015

company_info.headquarters: 1730 W Olympic Blvd #102 D, Los Angeles, CA 90015

pros: Accepts businesses with less than one year of operating history
Wide funding range from $1,000 to over $500,000
Quick qualification assessment through 3-step application process
Dedicated loan specialist assignment for personalized guidance
Accepts applicants at various business lifecycle stages (less than 1 year to 7+ years)
Low minimum funding tier ($1,000) accessible to very small businesses
cons: Website provides zero information on actual interest rates, APR, or loan terms
No disclosure of fees, origination charges, or repayment periods
Automatic qualification denials triggered for certain funding/revenue combinations without explanation
No visible regulatory licensing, BBB rating, or consumer review information accessible from contact page
Limited transparency about specific loan product types offered (SBA vs. merchant cash advance vs. lines of credit)
best_for: Early-stage business owners with less than 2 years of operating history seeking rapid funding decisions
Small businesses needing funding between $5,000-$100,000 without access to traditional bank loans
Business owners who prefer phone or text communication over in-person banking relationships
diagnosis: Fast Business Financial is positioned for early-stage business owners and small businesses that have been denied by traditional banks, particularly those needing funding quickly. The primary caveat is the complete absence of transparent loan terms, pricing, or regulatory information on their public website—applicants should independently verify licensing, research customer reviews, and obtain written loan terms before committing to any application.
services: Small business loan applications and processing
Quick qualification assessment based on business metrics
Text message marketing and communication opt-in
Online application submission via contact form
